title: Rule or Law?
short_description: A rule is made by people around you; a law is made by government for everyone — read each statement and sort it into the right box.
description: What's the difference between a rule and a law? A rule is made by the people around you — family, class, a game — to keep things fair, while a law is made by the government for the whole community and everyone must follow it. Read each statement and sort it into the right box. It's a core early-civics idea about how we live together, and grade 4–5 adds a second round on the difference between your rights and your responsibilities. Grade bands ramp from clear rule-or-law examples, to trickier ones, to a mix that includes rights and responsibilities. A free online civics, rules-and-laws and citizenship game for kids in grades 1–5, with streak bonuses feeding your score and GP.
controls: Mouse / tap the box (Rule or Law, Right or Responsibility) each statement belongs in.
